entries.source: For this mode, the job is simple: Improve writing with planning, revision, structure, and feedback that keeps your voice intact.

entries.source: Good prompts usually include three things: the subject, your current level, and the kind of help you want. You do not need perfect wording. You only need enough context for the session to begin.
entries.value: Good promptiau usually include three things: y subject, eich current level, a y kind of cymorth chi eisiau. chi do nid angen perfect wording. chi only angen enough context ar gyfer y session to begin.

entries.source: If the response feels too broad, narrow it. Ask for one example, one misconception, one check question, or one next step. If it feels too easy, ask for a challenge. If it feels too hard, ask for a bridge from what you already know.
entries.value: Os y response feels too broad, narrow it. gofyn ar gyfer un example, un misconception, un gwirio cwestiwn, neu un next cam. os it feels too easy, gofyn ar gyfer challenge. os it feels too hard, gofyn ar gyfer bridge o beth chi already know.

entries.source: Reading an AI response is not the same as learning. After the first answer, ask the mode to make you do something with the idea.

entries.source: That final recap is important. When you explain the idea back, you reveal what is solid and what is still borrowed from the AI.

entries.source: The best learning sessions are adjustable. The coach gives targeted revision priorities and before-after examples without replacing your voice.

entries.source: The fastest way to get value from Writing Coach is to give the AI a learning job, not just a topic. A topic says what you are interested in. A learning job says what kind of help you need.

entries.source: Use Writing Coach when this is the right mode for the job. If you want a related path, try Science Lab Partner. You can also browse the AI learning blog for study methods, Socratic learning, flashcards, roleplay, and active recall.
